summary.name: Intraday Contrarian Negative News Rebound
summary.author: Derek Melchin

summary.description_full:
The strategy is an intraday, event driven contrarian news signal applied to large cap Nasdaq equities. It monitors real time Tiingo news for the top 10 constituents of the Nasdaq 100 proxy (QQQ) and assigns a simple lexical sentiment score to each incoming article based on the presence of a small set of positive and negative keywords in the article description. The intended edge is short term mean reversion: stocks experiencing explicitly negative headline language are assumed to be temporarily oversold or subject to liquidity driven overreaction, creating a near term rebound opportunity.

When a news item arrives during regular market hours and the asset is continuously open (now and one minute prior), any stock with a negative score becomes eligible for purchase immediately. At each such event, the portfolio is rebalanced into an equal weighted basket of all currently flagged names, targeting 1 divided by N per name; names not in the current negative news set are implicitly reduced or exited through the rebalance. The universe is refreshed via QQQ constituents; any security removed from the universe is liquidated. Risk controls are limited to equal weighting and universe size constraints; there are no explicit stop losses, holding period limits, or volatility based sizing rules. Decisions occur on each news update rather than on a fixed bar interval.

raw.name: Intraday Contrarian Negative News Rebound

author.name: Derek Melchin
author.bio:
Quantitative Developer at QuantConnect. Studied Computer Science and Finance at the University of Lethbridge. Competitor in the 2020-1 Rotman International Trading Competitions. 
See my latest posts at derekmelchin.com.
